courseleaf.title: Linguistics, Doctor of Philosophy
courseleaf.informationtext:
Director of Graduate Studies: contact the Department of Linguistics; Faculty

Graduate Study

The Department of Linguistics offers a program of study leading to M.A. and Ph.D. degrees.

The Ph.D. degree offers advanced training and research in linguistic theories and methods. Second language acquisition and development is a particular emphasis of the program. Detailed information on both the M.A. and the Ph.D. degrees can be obtained from the graduate advisor, from the chair of the Graduate Group or the departmental chair.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Linguistics.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Linguistics at UC Davis distinguishes itself  by offering an area of emphasis in second language acquisition and development, which responds to a growing need for researchers trained to investigate multilingualism and the acquisition and teaching of nonnative languages. Other areas of emphasis include; language and society, language and mind/brain, and language structure and theory. The Ph.D. program is supported by a wide array of faculty and encompasses numerous research areas.
